controls: Dynamic strings become text nodes.
Raw markup requires the explicit unsafeHTML() escape hatch.
Renderer-owned innerHTML, outerHTML, and textContent property sinks are rejected.
areas.residualRisk: unsafeHTML() is not a sanitizer; applications own validation and sanitization of untrusted markup.

controls: Dynamic URL attributes and properties reject javascript:, vbscript:, and data: after control-character normalization.
Unsafe protocols require the explicit unsafeURL() escape hatch.
String event-handler attributes are rejected.
areas.residualRisk: The runtime is not an application URL allowlist or navigation policy; unsafeURL() bypasses protocol checks.

controls: Browser styling uses constructable CSSStyleSheet objects and adoptedStyleSheets.
SSR style carriers escape closing style tags and are removed only after manifest validation succeeds.
Style manifests preserve order, content identity, and digest validation.
areas.residualRisk: css() accepts author-controlled stylesheet source and is not a CSS sanitizer.

controls: SSR state accepts JSON-compatible data only.
Serialization escapes ampersand, angle brackets, U+2028, and U+2029 before embedding in an application/json script.
Every request owns its app, Router, Store manager, reactive scope, and serialized snapshot.
areas.residualRisk: Applications must not place secrets in browser-visible hydration state.

controls: The renderer transports a request-owned nonce to initial style carriers; module scripts remain external asset URLs.
The renderer never invents, reuses, or weakens a Content Security Policy.
Static resource URLs are escaped before entering generated markup.
areas.residualRisk: The deploying application owns the policy header, nonce generation, hash allowlists, and report handling.

controls: Gluon exposes explicit unsafeHTML() and unsafeURL() review points rather than silently trusting strings.
Version 1.0.10 makes no compatibility claim for require-trusted-types-for enforcement.
areas.residualRisk: Template parsing and explicit raw-markup paths use browser HTML sinks; an enforcing application must supply and review its own compatible Trusted Types policy until Gluon defines a public policy contract.

controls: The optional adapter accepts only absolute same-origin route paths and root-relative asset and hydration URLs.
Every render receives an isolated AbortSignal and request owner; success, failure, and external abort all execute deterministic disposal.
The adapter transports SSR style carriers, state, and resource hints unchanged and escapes optional CSP and hydration-entry attributes.
areas.residualRisk: Applications own Eleventy data trust, CSP policy correctness, custom document output, deployment fallback routing, and asset-manifest provenance.

controls: The CLI realpath-checks one root, skips symbolic links and generated or dependency directories, and applies fixed file and byte limits.
Application modules are parsed as inert source and are never imported, executed, transformed, or written.
The report contains project-relative locations and explicit exact, structural, or indeterminate confidence without timestamps or host data.
areas.residualRisk: Static evidence cannot prove dynamic route, Store, style, SSR, or runtime behavior; indeterminate records require application review.

controls: The analyzer reads only realpath-checked files below one root and never follows symbolic links.
Project application, configuration, plugin, loader, test, dependency, and package-script code is parsed as inert input and never executed.
Fixed file, byte, AST, depth, worker-memory, and worker-time budgets stop unbounded analysis; reports omit excerpts, absolute paths, host data, environment data, and timestamps.
The package exposes no source writer, transform, evaluator, visitor plugin, network, or telemetry hook.
areas.residualRisk: Static syntax inventory cannot establish arbitrary runtime behavior or semantic migration equivalence; indeterminate and unsupported findings require human review.

controls: The add-component planner validates the complete mutation set before writes and rejects absolute paths, traversal, NUL bytes, malformed manifests, invalid names and tags, and symbolic-link path segments.
Generated source and test collisions require an overwrite option plus a separate confirmation; dry-run returns the complete operation inventory without mutation.
Writes use same-directory temporary files and restore pre-write file contents if the commit phase fails.
areas.residualRisk: The generator updates only documented package fields and its marked barrel region; applications must review generated code and retain ownership of architecture, dependency policy, and deployment security.
